settings.tagline: Daily stock picks for independent traders
settings.context: Iran peace deal signed - FOMC decision Wednesday - rate hike probability falls to 2%

news.title: US and Iran reach peace deal - Strait of Hormuz set to reopen within 30 days
news.desc: The US and Iran agreed on a framework peace deal over the weekend, with the Strait of Hormuz reopening within 30 days as a key provision. Oil prices retreated on the news, Treasury yields pulled back, and S&P 500 futures are up this morning. This directly removes the two biggest headwinds from last week's crash - geopolitical risk premium in oil and the associated rate-hike fears that came with elevated energy prices.

news.title: SpaceX (SPCX) IPO surges 19% in debut - largest IPO in history at $75B
news.desc: SpaceX debuted on NASDAQ Friday at $135, closing at $160 - a 19% first-day gain - with market cap crossing $2 trillion. The overhang from investors raising cash for the IPO is now cleared, removing a key source of selling pressure that weighed on tech stocks all week. Five companies including Rocket Lab and CoreWeave were simultaneously added to the Nasdaq-100 in the June rebalancing.

news.title: Fed rate hike probability falls to 2% - markets now price 98% chance of hold on June 17
news.desc: Rate hike expectations cooled significantly heading into this week. Bloomberg rate probabilities show the first theoretical 100% probability of a hike has moved out to March 2027, from December 2026 last week. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh holds his first press conference Wednesday - his tone on inflation will be the key signal for the rest of 2026.

events.title: FOMC Rate Decision + Fed Chair Warsh Press Conference - June 17
events.desc: Wednesday's Fed decision is the most important event of the week. Markets are pricing 98% probability of a hold. The real event is new Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's inaugural press conference - his tone on inflation and future rate trajectory will set the market's expectations for the rest of 2026. A less hawkish tone than feared could trigger a sharp relief rally in beaten-down quality names.
events.tag: Highest impact event of the week

events.title: Bank of Japan Rate Decision - June 16
events.desc: The Bank of Japan announces policy tomorrow. Most analysts expect a hold. A surprise rate hike would strengthen the yen, trigger unwinding of global yen carry trades, and create risk-off pressure across global markets - the same dynamic that caused the August 2024 volatility spike. Low probability but high impact if it occurs.

events.title: US Retail Sales + Industrial Production - June 15 & 17
events.desc: May Industrial Production releases today. May Retail Sales drop on June 17 - the same morning as the Fed decision. Weak retail sales would signal consumer stress and increase pressure on the Fed to remain dovish. Strong retail sales give the Fed room to stay firm. This data directly shapes how markets interpret whatever Warsh says at 2pm Wednesday.

picks.desc: FTI Consulting provides restructuring, litigation, and economic consulting to major corporations globally. Down 33% from recent highs. Business fundamentals remain strong with growing demand across all divisions. Analyst targets $165-$185. Counter-cyclical business that historically performs well in periods of economic uncertainty.

World's highest-quality gold mining operations across Canada, Australia and Finland. Q1 2026 revenue grew 27% year-on-year and free cash flow grew 128%. Analyst consensus price target $199-$217 versus current ~$103. Pays a growing dividend. Gold prices remain structurally elevated globally.

Charter is the second largest cable and broadband operator in the US serving 32 million customers. Down 50% from 2025 highs. $25 billion share buyback program active at current prices. Broadband remains essential infrastructure with strong pricing power. Analyst targets $185-$220.

BigBear.ai provides AI-powered decision intelligence platforms to US defence and intelligence agencies. Revenue growing with expanding government contracts. Key product validation milestone scheduled July 1, 2026. Analyst consensus target $5.33.

Red Cat makes US-manufactured autonomous drones for defence applications with no Chinese components. Recently completed a $225M capital raise strengthening the balance sheet. All four covering analysts rate Strong Buy with targets of $19-$21. Active US Army contracts currently in production.
picks.warn: Large holder sale filing June 12 - confirm volume stabilisation before entering
